macro_events.event_summary: Oil futures rally after the Strait of Hormuz is closed again amid breakdown in peace talks, threatening ~20% of global seaborne oil flows.
primary_drivers: Supply shock premium priced into crude (Brent/WTI spike)
Shipping disruption and rerouting around the chokepoint
Risk-off flows into defense and safe havens

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with ~2.4M bpd upstream production largely outside the Gulf; realized crude price rises directly flow to upstream earnings while its limited Hormuz transit exposure shields it.
Upstream-heavy major; every $10 sustained Brent move adds roughly $4B to annual operating cash flow. Minimal Hormuz shipping exposure post-divestitures.
Pure-play Permian producer (Diamondback) — 100% US onshore, no Middle East exposure, highest beta to WTI among large E&Ps.
Cheniere — US LNG exporter. Hormuz closure cuts Qatari LNG (~20% of global LNG) from reaching Asia/Europe, redirecting demand and spot pricing to US Gulf Coast cargoes.
Scorpio Tankers — product tanker rates spike on forced rerouting around Africa, extending ton-miles materially. Direct rate beneficiary.
TechnipFMC — subsea/offshore services provider benefits as sustained high prices accelerate non-OPEC offshore FIDs; also peer-rotation proxy in oil services.
Lockheed — escalation in the Gulf raises probability of expanded US military posture and missile-defense (THAAD, PAC-3) replenishment orders; defense names rally on Hormuz headlines historically.

losers.rationale: Delta — jet fuel is ~25% of opex and unhedged since 2022; sustained crude spike compresses margins directly. Also trans-Atlantic route exposure to any broader conflict.
United — similar jet fuel exposure to DAL plus heavier international long-haul mix where rerouting and insurance premiums bite hardest.
Southwest — domestic carrier but fuel is the largest controllable cost; legacy hedge book largely rolled off. High beta to crude spikes.
Carnival — bunker fuel costs spike and Middle East/Red Sea itineraries get canceled (repeat of 2024 Houthi playbook), forcing costly rerouting.
Dow Inc. — ethane/naphtha feedstock costs rise with oil; limited ability to pass through in weak chemical demand environment compresses spreads.
Walmart — gasoline price spike acts as consumer tax, pressuring discretionary basket size; diesel also raises inbound logistics costs.
FedEx — jet fuel and diesel are major cost lines; surcharge pass-through lags by 4-6 weeks, creating margin compression in the interim.

headlines: Oil futures climb after Strait of Hormuz closed again as peace talks thrown into uncertainty

macro_events.event_summary: Market commentary highlights that a single company (overwhelmingly likely NVIDIA) is responsible for roughly half of S&P 500 earnings revisions, underscoring extreme concentration risk in the current rally.
primary_drivers: Earnings concentration risk — breadth deterioration raises fragility of index-level gains
Narrative reinforcement of AI capex cycle being the sole EPS growth engine
Potential positioning rotation warning for risk-parity and equal-weight strategies

beneficiaries.rationale: The article's framing — one company driving ~50% of S&P 500 EPS revisions — points directly to NVIDIA, whose datacenter revenue guide-ups have been the single largest contributor to index EPS since 2023. Reinforced narrative attracts incremental momentum/benchmark-chasing flows.

headlines: The narrow foundations of the current rally — one company is responsible for half of S&P 500 earnings revisions
